title: Profile scanner, libnorn resolution, and CLI delegation
purpose: Profile resolution is currently CLI-only (norn-cli profile_loader.rs) and only supports TOML/JSON at ~/.norn/config/profiles/. Sub-agents need to load profiles by name at runtime from within libnorn (SpawnAgentTool lives in libnorn, not norn-cli). This brief implements two-tier directory scanning in libnorn, changes the profile directory to ~/.norn/profiles/, adds .meridian/profiles/ as an additional scan tier, and delegates the CLI's profile_loader to the new libnorn API.
task: Implement a Scanner in norn::profile::loader that walks workspace-level ({cwd}/.norn/profiles/) then user-level (~/.norn/profiles/) directories, with .meridian/profiles/ as an additional tier. Workspace profiles win on name collision. The scanner finds .md files (primary) and falls back to .toml/.json for backwards compatibility. Implement resolve_profile(name, scan_dirs) as a libnorn API. Update norn-cli paths.rs to return ~/.norn/profiles/ instead of ~/.norn/config/profiles/. Update norn-cli profile_loader.rs to delegate to the libnorn scanner instead of its own file-based resolution.

requirements.title: Profile Scanner in libnorn
requirements.spec: The profile Scanner SHALL walk a list of directories in priority order, returning the first matching profile file for a given name.
acceptance: Scanner struct in profile/loader.rs with scan_dirs: Vec<PathBuf>
Scanner::new(scan_dirs) constructs from an ordered list of directories
Scanner::resolve(name) -> Option<PathBuf> finds the first match across directories
For each directory, checks: {name}.md first, then {name}.toml, then {name}.json
First directory with a match wins (workspace beats user)
Scanner::list_profiles() -> Vec<String> returns all available profile names (deduplicated, first-directory-wins)
Test: two directories with same profile name, first directory wins
Test: .md preferred over .toml in same directory

requirements.title: Two-tier scanning with Meridian fallback
requirements.spec: Profile resolution SHALL check workspace-level first, then user-level, with .meridian/profiles/ as an additional scan tier for Meridian-integrated workspaces.
acceptance: default_scan_dirs(cwd) -> Vec<PathBuf> returns: [{cwd}/.norn/profiles/, {cwd}/.meridian/profiles/, ~/.norn/profiles/]
Workspace .norn/profiles/ has highest priority
.meridian/profiles/ is second (Meridian integration)
~/.norn/profiles/ is the user-level fallback
Non-existent directories are silently skipped (no error)
Test: profile found in .meridian/profiles/ when not in .norn/profiles/

requirements.title: resolve_profile as libnorn API
requirements.spec: Profile resolution SHALL be available as a libnorn API so SpawnAgentTool can load profiles by name at runtime without depending on norn-cli.
acceptance: pub fn resolve_profile(name: &str, scan_dirs: &[PathBuf]) -> Result<Profile, ConfigError> in profile/loader.rs
Uses Scanner internally to find the file path
Calls Profile::from_file on the resolved path (supports .md, .toml, .json)
Returns ConfigError when profile not found (lists searched directories)
Test: resolve_profile finds a markdown profile by bare name
Test: resolve_profile returns error with searched paths when not found

requirements.title: CLI profile path and delegation
requirements.spec: norn-cli SHALL delegate profile resolution to libnorn's scanner and update the profiles directory path.
acceptance: paths.rs profiles_dir() returns ~/.norn/profiles/ instead of ~/.norn/config/profiles/
profile_loader.rs resolve_profile delegates to norn::profile::loader::resolve_profile for bare names
Explicit path resolution (contains / or .) still calls Profile::from_file directly
Default profile construction (no --profile flag) unchanged
Test: bare name resolves through libnorn scanner (using tempdir with .md profile)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement profile parsing — that is NA-001
SHALL NOT modify SpawnAgentTool — that is NA-006
SHALL NOT remove support for explicit file path profile loading (./path/to/profile.toml)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn -p norn-cli — all tests pass
Scanner resolves profile from workspace .norn/profiles/ — verified by test
Scanner resolves from .meridian/profiles/ when not in .norn/ — verified by test
Workspace profile overrides user-level on name collision — verified by test
CLI bare name resolves through libnorn scanner — verified by test
Existing CLI path-based resolution still works — verified by existing test
